folded_sections.from: What Makes This Better Than Markdown Export

pullquotes: The goal is not prettier notes. The goal is notes that pull you back into the thinking.
agent_decisions.doc_types_sections_treatment: Sections list rendered inline within each .doc-type description (interpunct-separated), rather than as a separate sub-component. Preserves source content without restructuring the existing .doc-type grid.
agent_decisions.non_goals_treatment: Five bullets folded into a single sage-toned callout with a clean ul, rather than five separate callouts.
agent_decisions.open_questions_treatment: Source markdown has questions without answers. Rendered as details/summary with short agent-supplied answers drawn from the working thesis and design doc, so a future-me reopening this artifact gets a starting point. First details is open by default.
agent_decisions.title_treatment: Hero H1 set to 'HTML Doc Kit — Vision.' (em dash, trailing period) for typographic warmth. Source H1 was 'HTML Doc Kit - Vision' (hyphen).
agent_decisions.notes: First doc rendered through /doc. Style anchor: ../../vision.html (hand-built). The hand-built artifact omits 7 of 13 ## sections; this render preserves all 13 via folds. Diff candidates worth resolving in V1: (1) should /doc auto-link the hero card meta values from the source's frontmatter rather than hand-pick them, (2) the .doc-type grid should grow a sections-list slot if doc-type-heavy docs become common, (3) Open Questions deserves a dedicated component when answers stay short.
